prompt: I'm spinning up a new React app and I want to use TanStack Router v1 (the current file-based routing one). Every time I let Claude touch routing it suggests react-router-dom code or weird v0 TanStack stuff it half-remembers. Can you set something up so future sessions actually follow v1 patterns? I care most about route definitions, loaders, and search params.

assertions.text: Pack directory exists at docs/references/tanstack-router-v1/ (or .claude/knowledge/ fallback)
INDEX.md exists with required frontmatter fields (title, concept, mode=framework, version, updated)
INDEX.md has a 'When to consult this pack' section that names concrete tasks (routing, loaders, search params)
INDEX.md has a 'Version-specific warnings' section listing at least 2 specific old-pattern -> new-pattern replacements
INDEX.md's Topics list has 3-10 topics and every bullet resolves to a real topic file
metadata.yaml exists and declares mode: framework
raw/ directory exists with downloaded source files from TanStack Router docs
At least one topic file includes a 'Common mistakes' block contrasting a training-data default against the correct pattern
CLAUDE.md has or grew a '## Framework/API References' section with a TanStack Router v1 bullet
The CLAUDE.md 'Consult when' phrase is specific — mentions routing/loaders/search-params rather than generic 'using TanStack Router'
